title: Media Services Live (MSL)
subtitle: Managed live streaming ingest and origin

steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: MSL is the satellite uplink truck at a live event. It receives the raw feed from the camera crew (encoder), packages it for broadcast, and hands it off to the delivery network (AMD/CDN). You focus on the event; MSL handles the plumbing.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
Media Services Live is Akamai's managed ingest and origin for live streams. Two generations:

- **MSL4**: Current production — SRT/RTMP ingest, HLS/DASH packaging, DRM token insertion, SCTE-35 passthrough, multi-CDN origin support
- **MSL5** (next-gen): Powered by Harmonic VOS360 — adds 24/7 linear playout, cloud nPVR, AI-powered ad marker insertion, automated sports clipping

MSL removes the need to run your own origin infrastructure for live events. Ingest an SRT/RTMP feed, MSL produces HLS/DASH manifests, AMD delivers.

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Live sports workflow: camera → encoder (on-site or cloud, e.g. Ateme) → SRT feed to MSL ingest point → MSL packages HLS/DASH with DRM tokens → AMD delivers to millions of viewers. For 24/7 linear channels (news, sports networks), MSL5 with Harmonic VOS360 handles continuous playout without custom origin infrastructure.
"MSL = managed live ingest and origin. Feed it SRT/RTMP, get HLS/DASH out. MSL5 (Harmonic VOS360) adds 24/7 playout and AI features."

quiz.question: What's the key difference between MSL4 and MSL5?

options.text: MSL5 is just MSL4 with a new name
options.explain: MSL5 is a fundamentally different platform, powered by Harmonic VOS360.

options.text: MSL5 is powered by Harmonic VOS360, adding 24/7 playout, cloud nPVR, and AI features like automated ad markers
options.explain: MSL5 expands from event-based live to full 24/7 linear operations with AI-powered automation.

options.text: MSL5 doesn't support live — only VOD
options.explain: MSL5 is specifically for live, including 24/7 linear.

options.text: MSL5 eliminates the need for an encoder
options.explain: You still need an encoder upstream. MSL handles ingest and origin.

quiz.question: A broadcaster needs managed live ingest for an SRT feed, producing HLS/DASH with DRM. What do they use?

options.explain: AMD is delivery (CDN). You need MSL for ingest and origin.

options.text: MSL for ingest + origin, AMD for CDN delivery
options.explain: MSL receives the SRT feed, packages HLS/DASH, inserts DRM tokens. AMD distributes globally.
Works for dev but not production live broadcasting. MSL is managed and reliable.
Object Storage can't do real-time ingest and packaging.
